title: UPDD: Universal Peptide Drug Discovery — v0.7.1 release for Paper 1 v1
description:
UPDD (Universal Peptide Drug Discovery) is a five-stage iterative pipeline for non-canonical amino acid (ncAA) peptide drug discovery, integrating generative protein design with quantum-mechanical binding validation on a single consumer-grade workstation.

This release accompanies the bioRxiv preprint Iterative Non-Canonical Amino Acid Peptide Drug Discovery via Sign-Direction Reproducible Evaluation: A Multi-Layer Verification Framework and contains the Stage-4 evaluation framework (ncAA parameterisation, restrained MD, QM/MM single-point, MM-PBSA scoring) integrated with established generation tools (RFdiffusion, ProteinMPNN, AlphaFold2). UPDD is positioned at Capability Level 1: a decision-support tool for ncAA variant ranking, not a quantitative experimental predictor.

Three methodological contributions are documented: the sigma_btwn / sigma_w ensemble-quality decomposition with the Convergence Index dual interpretation (binding-mode classifier + automated quality-control auditor); the branched delta-delta-G architecture with bit-identical wild-type control; and the four-layer onion-peeling verification protocol (closed-shell charge bias, periodic-boundary serialisation, intra-residue bond integrity for HETATM ncAAs, source-versus-test consistency). The release also ships a five-module Capability Level 1 suite and a simulated active-learning cycle on the six-target ncAA benchmark.
